core_model.structure_summary: A multivariate continuous-time Markov jump process on integer occupation counts governed by a master equation with bilinear (mass-action) forward rates and linear reverse rates under a conserved total, solved via a generating function that satisfies a first-order linear transport PDE with a multinomial stationary distribution.
core_model.canonical_form: \frac{dP_{\mathbf n}}{dt}=\sum_i\big[T(\mathbf n\mid\mathbf n+\mathbf e_i)P_{\mathbf n+\mathbf e_i}+T(\mathbf n\mid\mathbf n-\mathbf e_i)P_{\mathbf n-\mathbf e_i}-(T(\mathbf n+\mathbf e_i\mid\mathbf n)+T(\mathbf n-\mathbf e_i\mid\mathbf n))P_{\mathbf n}\big],\; T(\mathbf n+\mathbf e_i\mid\mathbf n)\propto n_A n_{R_i},\; T(\mathbf n-\mathbf e_i\mid\mathbf n)=\nu_i n_i,\; n_A+\sum_i n_i=\text{const};\quad G(\mathbf z,t)=\sum_{\mathbf n}P_{\mathbf n}z^{\mathbf n},\; \partial_t G=\sum_i[(1-z_i)\nu_i+z_i m(\mathbf z)]\partial_{z_i}G - n_A^0 m(\mathbf z)G

variables.meaning: vector of nonnegative integer occupation counts across labeled compartments
continuous time of the Markov process
conserved total count constraining the state simplex

notes: Core is the chemical-master-equation / birth-death Markov process for the count vector; the generating-function first-order linear PDE and its characteristic ODEs (including the Riccati dm/dt=-m^2+Qm+... ) are the solution machinery, and the multinomial P_n is the stationary law. Parameter-redundancy/identifiability results are downstream of this stochastic model. Forward rate is bilinear in two count species (multiplicative_interaction / mass_action).
